attack_surface.data_description: Signed claim transaction with recipient address and allocation proof/identifier.

security_gap.gap_description: The system may not adequately verify the claimant's right to the allocation.

actor_risk.description: An external user attempting to claim assets without proper authorization.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Exploiting this flaw leads to unauthorized asset transfer.

assumptions: The airdrop claim contract or off-chain service fails to adequately verify the user's entitlement to claim the specific allocation.

mitigation.strategy: Implement strong authentication and authorization checks based on cryptographically verifiable proof of allocation ownership.

description: An attacker impersonates a legitimate user to claim their BERA airdrop allocation.

origin.capability_match: Initiate transaction, Sign transaction, Approve ERC20

attack_surface.data_description: Signed swap transaction including trade pair, amounts, slippage, recipient.

security_gap.gap_description: Logic vulnerability in BEX smart contract allows unauthorized actions or asset manipulation.

actor_risk.description: An external user interacting with the core exchange contract with potential to exploit vulnerabilities.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Exploiting BEX contract vulnerabilities can lead to significant asset loss and market disruption.

assumptions: The BEX smart contract contains an exploitable logic vulnerability (e.g., re-entrancy, incorrect accounting, access control flaw).

mitigation.strategy: Conduct comprehensive smart contract audits and formal verification.

description: An attacker exploits a vulnerability in the BEX smart contract to steal funds from liquidity pools, execute unauthorized trades, or manipulate pool parameters.

trace_source.matched_capability: Initiate transaction, Sign transaction, Approve ERC20

origin.capability_match: Initiate transaction, Sign transaction, Provide proof

attack_surface.data_description: Signed claim transaction including proof data (e.g., Merkle proof), claimant address, and claim details.

security_gap.gap_description: Flaw in off-chain proof generation/distribution or on-chain validation allows claiming unearned or other users' rewards.

actor_risk.description: An external user attempting to claim rewards without proper authorization.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Exploiting the claiming mechanism directly leads to theft of user funds.

assumptions: The off-chain incentive proof service generates flawed proofs, proofs can be tampered with, or the on-chain contract validates proofs incorrectly allowing unauthorized claims.
An attacker can obtain or fabricate a valid proof for rewards they are not entitled to.

mitigation.strategy: Ensure secure off-chain proof generation and distribution, implement robust on-chain proof validation logic strictly tied to the claimant's identity.

description: An attacker obtains or fabricates a malicious proof to claim incentive tokens or BGT rewards belonging to other users or claim more tokens than earned.

trace_source.matched_capability: Initiate transaction, Sign transaction, Provide proof

origin.capability_match: Initiate transaction, Sign transaction, Define proposal

attack_surface.data_description: Signed transaction to create a proposal with title, description, and encoded call data.

security_gap.gap_description: Governance contract susceptible to spam or resource exhaustion via proposal creation.

actor_risk.description: An external user with sufficient BGT to create proposals.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Disrupts the governance process but doesn't directly lead to asset loss (unless governance controls are exploitable).

assumptions: The governance contract does not adequately rate-limit proposals or handle malicious proposal content efficiently.
The cost of creating spam proposals is low relative to the disruption caused.

mitigation.strategy: Implement rate limiting on proposal creation per address and ensure efficient contract handling of proposal data.

description: An attacker creates a large number of spam or resource-intensive proposals to disrupt the governance process, preventing legitimate proposals or votes.

trace_source.matched_capability: Initiate transaction, Sign transaction, Define proposal

origin.capability_match: Initiate transaction, Sign transaction, Approve ERC20, Set incentive rate

attack_surface.data_description: Signed transaction including target vault, incentive token address, amount, and rate.

security_gap.gap_description: Weak access control allows non-managers to add incentives or managers to add unapproved tokens/rates.

actor_risk.description: An external user who may not be the designated Token Manager attempting to add incentives.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Unauthorized adding of incentives disrupts validator economics and can lead to financial loss.

assumptions: The Reward Vault Management contract does not correctly verify that the transaction initiator is the designated Token Manager for the specified vault/token.
The mapping of authorized managers to tokens/vaults is incorrect or vulnerable to manipulation.

mitigation.strategy: Implement strict role-based access control in the contract, ensuring only authorized Token Managers can add incentives for their designated tokens/vaults.

description: An attacker impersonates a Token Manager or exploits an access control vulnerability to add malicious or misconfigured incentive tokens to a Reward Vault, disrupting validator rewards and harming legitimate users.

trace_source.matched_capability: Initiate transaction, Sign transaction, Approve ERC20, Set incentive rate

origin.capability_match: Initiate transaction, Sign transaction, Approve ERC20

attack_surface.data_description: Signed mint transaction including collateral type, amount, and desired HONEY amount.

security_gap.gap_description: Logic vulnerability in HoneySwap contract or associated vaults allows minting HONEY without sufficient/valid collateral or at incorrect rates.

actor_risk.description: An external user interacting with the stablecoin minting contract with potential to exploit vulnerabilities.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Exploiting minting contract vulnerabilities can lead to system insolvency and loss of user collateral.

assumptions: The HoneySwap or Vault smart contracts contain exploitable logic (e.g., incorrect calculation of minted HONEY, re-entrancy with collateral tokens).
The system relies on an insecure price feed for collateral valuation (if applicable).

mitigation.strategy: Conduct comprehensive smart contract audits and formal verification, ensure secure collateral handling and minting calculations.

description: An attacker exploits a vulnerability in the HoneySwap minting contract to mint HONEY without providing adequate collateral or manipulate minting/burning rates, potentially causing bad debt or instability.

trace_source.matched_capability: Initiate transaction, Sign transaction, Approve ERC20

attack_surface.data_description: Data displayed to the user about validators, vaults, portfolio, etc.

security_gap.gap_description: The off-chain service or UI serving public data is compromised, allowing data to be tampered with before presentation.

actor_risk.description: An external user consuming data from off-chain services.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Misleading users can cause them to make incorrect decisions or interact with malicious contracts.

assumptions: The off-chain service (UI, API) serving public data is vulnerable to compromise or data manipulation.
The communication channel between the service and the user is not secured.

mitigation.strategy: Implement secure coding practices for services, use HTTPS, and advise users to verify critical information on-chain explorer.

description: An attacker compromises the BeraHub/BEX UI or backend service to display misleading information (e.g., false APRs, incorrect claimable amounts), potentially tricking users into harmful actions.
